programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny meets people making life-changing moves from city to country. James sets out to create a boutique country hotel, but is that the best way to earn a new living.

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Thanks to his new catchphrase, 'I didn't do it', Bart becomes famous, getting his own TV series and releasing a range of merchandise.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Apu loses the Kwik-E-Mart, he moves in with the Simpsons. And then Homer joins him on a pilgrimage to the first ever Kwik-E-Mart, in the Himalayas.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: He may be obscenely rich, but Mr Burns can never be truly happy without his childhood teddy bear, Bobo, who Bart finds in a bag of ice and gives to Maggie.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie can't stop buying expensive clothes and then taking them back to the shops within a few days. Spence's new girlfriend is terrified of his dog.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug thinks he might finally be able to get his father-in-law to move out when he discovers that Arthur has inherited a house nearby.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: When Carrie agrees to Arthur's choices of what to have for dinner and which movie to rent, Doug gets upset that he doesn't get his way.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray agrees to go back to church, he soon discovers why Frank is happy to attend every week.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Robert is injured in the line of duty, Ray realises that he is partly - although unwittingly - responsible.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Debra discovers that Ray taped over their wedding video with the Super Bowl, he has to go to extreme lengths to make it up to her.

programme.title: F1 Miami GP Sprint and Qualifying Highlights
programme.desc: There's no room for error in the second Sprint race of the season. Then the teams must refocus to secure the best possible starting position for the GP.

programme.title: Live: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show, with guests Russell Kane, Gregor Fisher, Greg McHugh, Lena Hall and Kerry Katona, and music from Tyler Ballgame.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and her new friend Ruth Powers set off on a Thelma and Louise-style adventure, with Chief Wiggum and Homer in hot pursuit..

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: During an all-syrup Squishee bender, Bart joins the junior campers, and ends up taking the embarrassing - and dangerous - Homer on a river rafting trip.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Tay House in Perth and Kinross, where the B&Bers aren't too happy about a lack of TVs. And there's something fishy going on at breakfast..

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Aberlour Hotel in Moray, where the B&Bers are in good spirits at a distillery. But there's a hangover at feedback time.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Visit three is to Cobblehouse Country Cabins in Aberdeenshire. Graham and Emma hope the B&Bers will be wowed by their wood. Will everyone be left pining for more.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Yuliya hosts the final visit at Trinity Boutique B&B in Peterhead. She takes her guests directly to jail (just visiting). But will she offer them more than just porridge at breakfast.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day brings cutting questions, cleanliness concerns and a Moroccan mismatch as the B&Bers find out who's won the week.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Liz dreams of owning a holiday home in her favourite part of Catalunya: Sitges. Will any of Jasmine Harman's options for £180k impress her enough to make an offer.

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George transforms a rusty lift into a cabin in the sky, and heads to Denmark to visit a modern Viking longhouse. Plus: the pinkest thing to ever appear on the show.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: Kate Garraway, Phil Wang, Gbemisola Ikumelo and Sophie Willan take on mini loaf cakes and reveal their hidden talents in showstopping choux.

programme.title: Cruising to the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: As the sparkling Diamond Princess glides into South Korea, guests enjoy the colourful Gamcheon Culture Village, while Alaskan cruisers witness the Mendenhall Glacier.

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: A former Birmingham City footballer sings for her grandad. A navy officer performs Nessun Dorma and brings the shoppers to a standstill. And Nat King Cole has everyone dancing.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers settle down for I'm a Celebrity... South Africa, Virgin Island, Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?, The Neighbourhood, Made in Chelsea and How to Clean-Up for Cash.

programme.title: Free Guy
programme.desc: Comedy action starring Ryan Reynolds. Guy lives in Free City. Free City isn't real. Nor is Guy. It's all a big online game. Can he take down the corrupt game publisher.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: What would happen if all the old people in Springfield turned into dinosaurs? Well, the answer lies in Mr Burns's new retirement home: Geriatric Park.

programme.title: F1 Miami GP Highlights
programme.desc: The drivers push their cars to the ragged edge of grip and control, battling each other and the Miami heat. Can anyone beat dominant Mercedes or will Antonelli make it three in a row.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Secret Service
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay infiltrates struggling restaurants with the help of a secret insider. Can he pull a dysfunctional diner back from the brink of bankruptcy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and her new friend Ruth Powers set off on a Thelma and Louise-style adventure, with Chief Wiggum and Homer in hot pursuit..

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: During an all-syrup Squishee bender, Bart joins the junior campers, and ends up taking the embarrassing - and dangerous - Homer on a river rafting trip.

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a one-pan salmon pie.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Dave and Lynn get to work on their dream farmhouse kitchen-diner, while Pete goes shopping for a tractor for his family's new smallholding.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Another chance to catch Friday's new episode, with Rev Kate Bottley in Dictionary Corner. Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam donates his old baseball shirt to a televised celebrity auction, but feels humiliated when no one bids for it.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane allows one of her psychology classmates to use the bar in an experiment about paranoia, but starts to feel paranoid waiting for the gang to get their revenge.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Frasier hasn't been the same since Diane dumped him in Europe, so she and Sam try to help him regain the confidence he needs to return to psychology.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray shows an interest in Ally's 'Hackidu' trading card hobby but inadvertently gives away her favourite 'Scramisaur' card.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra finally cooks a dish that Ray, Robert and Frank all enjoy, but Marie is not impressed with her efforts.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra plan to get more time to themselves by encouraging Marie and Frank to make new friends. But all does not go to plan.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Miracle on Third or Fourth Street: When his son Frederick decides to spend Christmas in Switzerland with Lilith, a dispirited Frasier looks set for a miserable festive season.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier upsets Martin when he reveals personal details about his love life during a radio broadcast.

programme.title: F1 Miami GP Highlights
programme.desc: The drivers push their cars to the ragged edge of grip and control, battling each other and the Miami heat. Can anyone beat dominant Mercedes or will Antonelli make it three in a row.

programme.title: The Kid Who Would Be King
programme.desc: Fantasy adventure. A 12-year-old from modern-day suburbia finds King Arthur's sword Excalibur and must save the country from a sinister sorceress. With Patrick Stewart.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Nina Wadia in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Bob and Vida want to buy a holiday bolthole on the west coast of the Peloponnese that one day could become their permanent home. Can Laura Hamilton find the perfect place for their £300k.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: New series following the fun-filled lives of Brits seeking a fresh start. The Best of British pub opens in Orlando. In Kissimmee, Anthony's busy cooking at his own birthday party.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Vivienne and Simon re-invent a tired room into an Empire suite. But with loads to do will the Empire strike back? And Tim and Sasha's bride goes missing.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, the Garners help friends Lynn and Della find a modern bolthole with a community feel. Charlotte Rodriguez treats Barry and Mandy to Alicante's sun-soaked options.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at Andy and Jess Tuck's grand renovation of their cramped three-bed semi in Long Eaton. Did they manage it on a shoestring budget.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Rocky the bulldog has had a much-needed facelift for his date, but only has eyes for a toy avocado. Muddy puddle enthusiast Grady the lurcher is looking for a new thrill.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: It's the Dating Phase of the course. As some of the virgins work to overcome their anxieties, Tegan struggles for self-acceptance. But for two members sparks really start to fly.

programme.title: Love Cheats: The Fake Lord
programme.desc: True crime series following extraordinary stories of romance fraud and the fight to bring con artists to justice. A fake lord cheats his victims out of thousands.

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: Peter has a fast-growing tumour and needs throat reconstruction to save his life. Janet's pancreatic cancer is wrapped around a vital vein - can the growth be removed.

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: A shocking account of the 2018 poisoning of ex-spy Sergei Skripal, with gripping first-hand testimony. Was Britain on the verge of a serious international conflict.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Secret Service
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay infiltrates struggling restaurants with the help of a secret insider. This time: inexperienced owner Steve's jumbled BBQ joint gets a grilling.

programme.title: Outrageous Homes
programme.desc: Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen checks out a vampire fantasy lair, a teeny-tiny cottage with huge impact, a home bursting with colour and a 1900s house with its own corner shop.

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programme.desc: George meets a family of five with a Victorian coach house that's more 1960s than 1860s, and helps a young couple in Hove get to grips with a 16th-century gatehouse.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Plymouth, Caroline and Steve plan a complex Middle Eastern menu. Jade and Seb make some schoolboy errors. And Bev and Nick serve up a shellfish platter, even though Bev hates seafood.

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: The fourth member of the Keane family arrives. Cleo struggles with guilt. Pearl tries to build bridges between torn families. And Diane confides in Tony and Leela.
